summary: Russian forces reported liberating Dobropasovo in Dnipropetrovsk region. Ukraine experienced a mass nighttime missile attack on Kyiv and surrounding areas, causing over 100 injuries and four deaths. Both sides continue aerial operations, including drone flights and missile launches across multiple fronts.
key_points: Russian troops liberated the village of Dobropasovo near Pokrovskoe in Dnipropetrovsk region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30415).
A mass nighttime attack on Ukraine injured over 100 people and killed four, targeting Kyiv and Kyiv region (Source: @UkraineNow/81439).
Russian forces struck Bila Tserkva with a medium‑range "Oreshnik" ballistic missile (Source: @UkraineNow/81439).
Ukrainian SBU successfully attacked a Russian fuel‑pump station supplying Moscow airports (Source: @UkraineNow/81439).
Russian Ministry of Defense advised foreigners to leave Kyiv and warned residents against approaching military and administrative sites (Source: @tass_agency/377478).

Multiple Russian‑controlled UAVs reported operating over Dnipropetrovsk, Kharkiv, Sumy, and Kyiv regions, including a reconnaissance UAV over the Black Sea (Source: @eRadarrua/93593, @eRadarrua/93613).
Russian officials publicly declared intent to continue strikes on Kyiv, legitimising further attacks (Source: @UkraineNow/81451).

sections.executive_summary: Russian forces liberated Dobropasovo in Dnipropetrovsk region, marking a territorial gain. Ukraine suffered a large‑scale nighttime missile barrage on Kyiv, with over 100 injured and four fatalities. Both sides maintain intensive aerial activity, including UAV flights and ballistic missile launches across several fronts.

points: Ukrainian authorities reported a massive nighttime missile attack that injured more than 100 civilians and killed four, with Kyiv and Kyiv oblast identified as primary targets (Source: @UkraineNow/81439).
The Ukrainian Security Service (SBU) carried out a successful strike on a Russian fuel‑pump station that supplies Moscow’s airports, disrupting logistics (Source: @UkraineNow/81439).
Ukrainian MP Aleksey Goncharenko disclosed damage to the apartments of President Zelensky and businessman Timur Mindich at 9A Grushevsky Street during the Kyiv attack (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30398).

Ukrainian defense advisor Sergey Beskrestnov confirmed Russian strikes on the Ukrainian Ground Forces command building and the Lukyanovka (Artem) missile‑production plant (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30410).
Ukrainian forces maintained control of the village of Mala Tokmachka in Zaporizhzhia region for over 1,500 days despite continuous Russian assaults (Source: @UkraineNow/81449).
The Russian Ministry of Defense announced the liberation of Dobropasovo village near Pokrovskoe in Dnipropetrovsk region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30415).
Russian officials warned foreign nationals to leave Kyiv promptly and cautioned residents against approaching military or administrative infrastructure (Source: @tass_agency/377478).

Russia’s defence ministry confirmed the use of the medium‑range "Oreshnik" ballistic missile against Ukrainian targets, including Bila Tserkva, and cited additional strikes with Iskander, Kinjal and Zircon missiles (Source: @UkraineNow/81431).
Russian spokesperson indicated that attacks on Kyiv would continue and were being carried out in response to Ukrainian actions (Source: @UkraineNow/81451).
A Russian‑controlled UAV was observed maneuvering from Bogdanivka toward the Dnipro/Solone area in Dnipropetrovsk region (Source: @eRadarrua/93563).
UAV groups were reported heading toward Izium and Balaklia in eastern Kharkiv region (Source: @kpszsu/62738).
A UAV flight from Kharkiv was directed northward across Poltava region, passing near Kotelva, heading southwest (Source: @kpszsu/62711).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aerial bombs over Kharkiv region (Source: @kpszsu/62725).
Another guided bomb sortie was reported over Sumy region (Source: @kpszsu/62736).
UAV activity was noted over the Dnipro‑Petrovsk area heading toward Vasylivka (Source: @kpszsu/62722).
Nine Shahed drones were reported moving from Chernihiv region toward Kyiv region, specifically Vyshedor district (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67218).
UAVs were observed over Chernihiv city and heading toward Nizhyn and Sosnytsia (Source: @eRadarrua/93578).
A UAV flight from Chernihiv passed near Slavutych heading south (Source: @kpszsu/62707).
UAVs were seen over the Kyiv reservoir at an altitude of 9,000 m, likely for reconnaissance (Source: @eRadarrua/93593).
A UAV group was reported over Kyiv, approaching from the north near Obukhiv (Source: @kpszsu/62729).
UAVs were observed moving from the Black Sea toward Odesa region (Source: @kpszsu/62742).
A Russian‑controlled UAV was reported over Mykolaiv region near Luperove and Snihurivka, heading to a training ground (Source: @eRadarrua/93575).
UAV activity was noted over Mykolaiv region near the Black Sea (Source: @eRadarrua/93613).
A Russian‑controlled UAV was seen over the Dnipro‑Petrovsk area heading toward Pavlograd (Source: @kpszsu/62723).
A UAV was reported over Mykolaiv region near the water reservoir (Source: @eRadarrua/93593).
Russian tactical aviation conducted guided bomb strikes on Kharkiv and Sumy regions (Source: @kpszsu/62725, @kpszsu/62736).
Ukrainian forces reported a missile strike on the Ukrainian Ground Forces command building and the Lukyanovka plant (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30410).
Multiple UAV flights were documented across the front lines, including reconnaissance over the Black Sea and various oblasts (Source: @eRadarrua/93613, @eRadarrua/93593).
Russian officials announced the use of the "Oreshnik" ballistic missile against Bila Tserkva and other targets (Source: @UkraineNow/81431).
A threat of ballistic weapon use from the south was announced by Russian sources (Source: @kpszsu/62685).

key_developments: Russian forces liberated Dobropasovo near Pokrovskoe in Dnipropetrovsk region, expanding their control (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30415).
A large‑scale nighttime missile attack on Kyiv and Kyiv oblast injured over 100 civilians and killed four, marking a significant escalation (Source: @UkraineNow/81439).
Russia employed the medium‑range "Oreshnik" ballistic missile against Bila Tserkva, demonstrating continued use of strategic weapons (Source: @UkraineNow/81439).
Ukrainian SBU successfully struck a Russian fuel‑pump station that supplies Moscow airports, disrupting enemy logistics (Source: @UkraineNow/81439).
Multiple Russian‑controlled UAVs were observed operating over Dnipropetrovsk, Kharkiv, Sumy, and Kyiv regions, indicating expanded drone activity (Source: @eRadarrua/93593, @eRadarrua/93613).
Russian defence ministry advised foreigners to leave Kyiv and warned residents against approaching military sites, reflecting heightened security concerns (Source: @tass_agency/377478).
Russian officials publicly declared intent to continue strikes on Kyiv, legitimising further attacks on the capital (Source: @UkraineNow/81451).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian forces are consolidating gains in the Dnipropetrovsk region while intensifying missile strikes on strategic Ukrainian targets, including Kyiv and Bila Tserkva. Ukrainian defensive actions, such as the SBU fuel‑pump attack, show capability to disrupt Russian logistics. The proliferation of UAV operations across multiple fronts increases the risk of rapid, low‑altitude attacks and reconnaissance, complicating air defence planning.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine has mobilised emergency services and reinforced civil defence in Kyiv following the mass missile barrage, while diplomatic channels highlight continued Western support for air‑defence enhancements. Russian statements urging foreign evacuation from Kyiv may aim to pressure international presence and limit diplomatic activity in the capital. Neighboring states remain vigilant, with no reported cross‑border troop movements at this time.
sections.intelligence_notes: Open‑source monitoring confirms increased UAV traffic from Russian‑held areas toward Ukrainian frontlines, suggesting a coordinated drone campaign. The use of the "Oreshnik" missile indicates Russia's willingness to employ medium‑range ballistic assets despite international scrutiny. Continued propaganda from Russian officials about ongoing attacks serves both domestic and strategic messaging purposes.
